name: The Boys /// RPG
description:
THE BOYS · AMAZON PRIME VIDEO · ERIC KRIPKE

THE BOYS

"With great power... comes the absolute certainty of becoming a right c**."

— Billy Butcher

The world has superheroes. That is not the problem. The problem is that Vought International got to them first. Fifty years of brand management, congressional lobbying, and very aggressive NDAs later, the most powerful beings alive are a product line. They have agents. They have film deals. They have merchandise. What they do not have is accountability, and Vought's legal team has spent half a century making sure that gap never closes.

It starts with Compound V. A synthetic serum injected into infants that creates permanent powers. Taken as Temp V by adults for a 24-hour window that burns something in the brain each time. The Supes it produces range from genuinely altruistic to catastrophically dangerous, and that difference has never moved Vought's stock price by more than a few points in either direction.

▸ THE SEVEN

Vought's flagship team. The most powerful Supes on the planet, contractually obligated, fully managed, and deployed according to a PR calendar. Homelander leads... flies, fires laser beams capable of leveling buildings, reads emotional states through heartbeat, and has been in psychological deterioration since before the show began. Starlight joined believing in what the job was supposed to be. Queen Maeve stopped believing long before the show starts and drinks through it. A-Train is fast and getting slower. The Deep has gills in places he doesn't discuss publicly. Black Noir is whatever Vought needs him to be this quarter.

Their internal psych evaluations are classified above the CEO level. Their public approval ratings are managed by a dedicated team of forty-three people. The gap between those two facts is the entire show.

▸ THE BOYS

Billy Butcher has a crowbar, a grudge that stopped being about his wife years ago. He built a team out of people who had nowhere else to go: Hughie Campbell, whose girlfriend A-Train ran through and kept moving. Mother's Milk, who never fully left. Kimiko, who Compound V was done to without her consent. Frenchie, who stayed for reasons he mostly keeps to himself. Later: Starlight, from the inside.

They have no powers(except starlight and kimiko and technically...Butcher?), no legal standing, no official existence. What they have is the knowledge that Vought's PR operation runs on the public not knowing what's in those sealed files, and a specific interest in making sure the files stop being sealed.

▸ FIVE SEASONS. ONE DIRECTION.

A-Train ran through a girl and kept moving. Stormfront was a uhhhh can I even say it on Janitor AI? She was a living ##### ##### experiment who'd
